title.zh: 量子真空的「雙人舞」：科學家首次捕捉到夸克自旋糾纏的瞬間
title.en: The Quantum Tango: Physicists Capture First Evidence of Entangled Quark Spins in the Vacuum

summary.zh:
在量子色動力學(QCD)的奇異世界中，真空並非空無一物，而是充滿了不斷漲落的能量場與虛粒子對的『量子泡沫』。由美國布魯克海文國家實驗室STAR團隊發表在《自然》期刊的最新研究，首次透過Λ超子對的相對極化測量，捕捉到虛夸克-反夸克對在強相互作用禁閉過程中的自旋關聯現象。

當高能質子對撞將真空中的虛夸克對『解放』出來，這些瞬間存在的粒子會帶著原始的自旋糾纏狀態，最終形成可觀測的Λ/反Λ超子。研究團隊測得(18±4)%的顯著極化相關性，且當超子對空間分離角度增大時，這種量子關聯會如預期般消失——這正是量子退相干的特徵。該發現不僅為『手徵對稱性破缺』與夸克禁閉的關聯機制提供新線索，更開創了用強子『化石』追溯真空量子態的實驗範式。
summary.en:
The quantum vacuum is far from empty—it's a seething sea of fluctuating energy fields and virtual particle-antiparticle pairs. A groundbreaking study by the STAR collaboration at Brookhaven's Relativistic Heavy Ion Collider, published in Nature, has now captured the first direct evidence of spin correlations between virtual strange quark pairs emerging from this quantum foam during QCD confinement.

By analyzing Λ-antiΛ hyperon pairs produced in high-energy proton collisions, physicists detected an (18±4)% relative polarization signal—a smoking gun indicating that the spins of transient quark-antiquark pairs remain entangled as they undergo hadronization. Crucially, this correlation disappears when hyperons are widely separated, demonstrating the expected quantum decoherence. The findings not only shed light on the mysterious link between chiral symmetry breaking and quark confinement but also establish a novel experimental approach to probe the vacuum's quantum structure through its hadronic 'fossils'.

keyArticle.title: Measuring spin correlation between quarks during QCD confinement
